prompt: I want to create a brand new KMP project from scratch called "WeatherPulse" — a weather forecast app targeting Android and iOS. Use Kotlin Multiplatform with Compose Multiplatform, Room for caching weather data, Ktor for API calls, Koin for DI, and Jetpack Navigation 3 for navigation. Please: 1) Initialize the project with proper Gradle structure (settings.gradle.kts, root build.gradle.kts, gradle/libs.versions.toml, gradle.properties), 2) Create a shared module with commonMain, androidMain, and iosMain source sets, 3) Create an androidApp module and iosApp directory, 4) Create project management files in a .skills/ directory (guide with phased execution, hints, validation plan), 5) Create an AGENTS.md with project instructions, 6) Set up Room database with a WeatherForecast entity, 7) Set up navigation with 3 screens: Home (current weather), Forecast (weekly), and Settings.
source: Greenfield KMP project created from empty directory
agent_rubric: A valid Gradle project structure exists: settings.gradle.kts, root build.gradle.kts, gradle/libs.versions.toml, and gradle.properties
A shared KMP module exists with build.gradle.kts applying kotlin.multiplatform and configuring androidTarget + iOS targets
The shared module has the standard KMP source set layout: commonMain, androidMain, iosMain
An Android app module exists (androidApp/) with build.gradle.kts applying com.android.application
An iOS app directory exists (iosApp/) with at least an Xcode project file or basic structure
An AGENTS.md file exists at project root with project-specific AI agent instructions
A .skills/ directory exists with a guide task file that has phased execution steps using **User Action**, **Agent Action**, and **Validation** prefixes
gradle/libs.versions.toml includes Room, Ktor, Koin dependencies with specific version numbers
Navigation uses Jetpack Navigation 3 (org.jetbrains.androidx.navigation3), NOT the older navigation-compose library
Room database is set up with a WeatherForecast entity using @Entity annotation in commonMain
Room database builder uses expect/actual pattern for platform-specific initialization
Navigation screens are implemented: Home, Forecast, and Settings with navigation between them
The project structure is internally consistent — imports and packages match across modules
